article.title: Drug-resistant Shigella infections on the rise
article.description: A new report from the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention shows that extensively drug resistant Shigella infections are increasing.The research, published in the CDC's Morbidity and Mortality Weekly Report, covered a sample of Shigella infections from 2011 through 2023. article.title: FDA warns New York firm for failing to verify safety of imported foods
article.description: As part of its enforcement activities, the Food and Drug Administration sends warning letters to entities under its jurisdiction. article.title: Scientists flag travel-related infection risks
article.description: An analysis of travel-related infections in England has found the odds of getting sick are higher when visiting countries outside Europe on all-inclusive packages.Having a better understanding of travel-associated infections could help to improve pre-travel advice and support public health actions, which may lead to a reduction in gastrointestinal

article.title: Companies fined after deaths at chicken factory
article.description: Two companies have been fined after two men were killed by faults involving a system used to reduce Campylobacter contamination at a chicken factory in England.Banham Poultry Ltd and Air Products PLC were sentenced at Norwich Crown Court after the deaths of Jonathan Collins, 34, and Neil Moon, 49,

article.title: Sunday Edition: Fowl plague
article.description: We now know that the 1918 influenza pandemic was caused by an H1N1 virus with genes of avian origin.
article.content: We now know that the 1918 influenza pandemic was caused by an H1N1 virus with genes of avian origin.

article.title: Oregon State launches online food safety training series

article.title: FDA increases import enforcement on certain cheese, seafood, cilantro
article.description: The Food and Drug Administration uses import alerts to enforce U.S. food safety regulations for food from foreign countries. The agency updates and modifies the alerts as needed.Recent modifications to FDA’s import alerts, as posted by the agency, are listed below.

article.title: Zaatar being recalled in Canada because of Salmonella
article.description: Alarjawi brand Royal Zaatar is being recalled in Canada because of contamination with Salmonella.The recalled product was distributed in Alberta, Nova Scotia, Ontario, Quebec and Saskatchewan. As of the posting of the recall by the Canadian Food Inspection Agency there had been no confirmed illnesses related to the product.
article.content: Alarjawi brand Royal Zaatar is being recalled in Canada because of contamination with Salmonella.The recalled product was distributed in Alberta, Nova Scotia, Ontario, Quebec and Saskatchewan. As of the posting of the recall by the Canadian Food Inspection Agency there had been no confirmed illnesses related to the product.

article.title: USDA issues public alert on beef and pork with undeclared sesame

article.title: Salmonella outbreak continues with 68 confirmed patients
article.description: The FDA is continuing to investigate an outbreak of Salmonella Newport infections that has sickened at least 68 people.The outbreak was first reported by the Food and Drug Administration on Feb. 25. At that time the agency reported 38 confirmed patients. The source of the outbreak is not yet

article.title: STOP Foodborne Illness to spotlight food safety culture at Food Safety Summit
article.description: By STOP Foodborne IllnessAs the industry prepares to gather in Rosemont, Illinois for the 28th annual Food Safety Summit, a longstanding partnership with STOP Foodborne Illness (STOP) will once again take center stage.
